sentences.sentence: Procrastination is the habit of delaying a necessary task, usually by focusing on less urgent, more enjoyable, and easier activities instead.
sentences.translation: 拖延是把必要任务推迟的习惯，通常转而关注不那么紧迫、更有趣也更容易的活动。

structure.text: the habit of delaying a necessary task
usually by focusing on less urgent, more enjoyable, and easier activities instead

difficult_points: of delaying 是动名词短语，说明 habit 的具体内容。
by focusing... 表示拖延所采取的方式，instead 表示转而做别的事。

sentences.sentence: We all do it from time to time.
sentences.translation: 我们都会时不时这样做。
difficult_points: do 代替前句的 procrastinate，避免重复。
it 指拖延这一行为。

sentences.sentence: We might be composing a message to a friend who we have to let down, or putting together an important report for college or work; we're doing our best to avoid doing the job at hand, but deep down we know that we should just be getting on with it.
sentences.translation: 我们可能正在给一个不得不让其失望的朋友写消息，或整理一份大学或工作中的重要报告；我们竭力逃避手头的任务，但内心深处知道自己其实应该继续把它做下去。

structure.text: We might be composing a message to a friend who we have to let down, or putting together an important report for college or work
who we have to let down
we're doing our best to avoid doing the job at hand
but deep down we know that we should just be getting on with it
that we should just be getting on with it

difficult_points: who 从句修饰 friend，let down 表示让朋友失望。
avoid 后接动名词 doing；get on with it 表示继续处理手头的事。
but 后 that 从句说明内心知道的内容。

sentences.sentence: Unfortunately, berating ourselves won't stop us procrastinating again.
sentences.translation: 遗憾的是，责骂自己并不能阻止我们再次拖延。
difficult_points: berating ourselves 是动名词短语作主语。
stop + 宾语 + 动名词表示阻止某人继续做某事。

sentences.sentence: In fact, it's one of the worst things we can do.
sentences.translation: 事实上，这是我们能做的最糟糕的事情之一。
difficult_points: we can do 是省略关系代词的定语从句，修饰 things。
it 指责骂自己的行为。

sentences.sentence: This matters because, as my research shows, procrastination doesn't just waste time, but is actually linked to other problems, too.
sentences.translation: 这很重要，因为正如我的研究显示，拖延不仅浪费时间，实际上还与其他问题有关。

structure.text: because, as my research shows, procrastination doesn't just waste time, but is actually linked to other problems, too
doesn't just waste time, but is actually linked to other problems, too

difficult_points: because 引导原因从句，as my research shows 是插入语。
not just...but... 连接 waste 和 is linked 两个谓语。

sentences.sentence: Contrary to popular belief, procrastination is not due to laziness or poor time management.
sentences.translation: 与普遍看法相反，拖延并非源于懒惰或糟糕的时间管理。

structure.text: is not due to laziness or poor time management

difficult_points: contrary to 表示“与……相反”，due to 引出原因。
or 连接 laziness 与 poor time management。

sentences.sentence: Scientific studies suggest procrastination is, in fact, caused by poor mood management.
sentences.translation: 科学研究表明，拖延事实上是由糟糕的情绪管理造成的。

structure.text: procrastination is, in fact, caused by poor mood management

difficult_points: suggest 后接省略 that 的宾语从句。
is caused by 是被动语态，poor mood management 是原因。

sentences.sentence: This makes sense if we consider that people are more likely to put off starting or completing tasks that they are really not keen to do.
sentences.translation: 如果考虑到人们更可能推迟开始或完成自己确实不愿做的任务，这就说得通了。

structure.text: if we consider that people are more likely to put off starting or completing tasks
that they are really not keen to do

difficult_points: if 从句中 consider 后接 that 宾语从句。
put off 后接两个并列动名词；that 修饰 tasks，be keen to do 表示愿意做。

sentences.sentence: If just thinking about the task threatens our sense of self-worth or makes us anxious, we will be more likely to put it off.
sentences.translation: 如果仅仅想到这项任务就威胁到我们的自我价值感或让我们焦虑，我们就更可能把它推迟。

structure.text: If just thinking about the task threatens our sense of self-worth or makes us anxious
will be more likely to put it off

difficult_points: just thinking about the task 是 if 从句的动名词主语。
threatens 与 makes 并列；put it off 中 it 指 task。

sentences.sentence: Research involving brain imaging has found that areas of the brain linked to detection of threats and emotion regulation are actually different in people who chronically procrastinate compared to those who don't procrastinate frequently.
sentences.translation: 涉及脑成像的研究发现，与不经常拖延的人相比，长期拖延者大脑中负责威胁检测和情绪调节的区域实际上有所不同。

structure.text: that areas of the brain linked to detection of threats and emotion regulation are actually different in people who chronically procrastinate compared to those who don't procrastinate frequently
linked to detection of threats and emotion regulation

difficult_points: linked to... 修饰 areas；who 从句修饰 people。
compared to those... 是比较结构，those 指不经常拖延的人。

sentences.sentence: Tasks that are emotionally loaded or difficult, such as preparing for exams, are prime candidates for procrastination.
sentences.translation: 情绪负担重或困难的任务，例如备考，是最容易被拖延的对象。

structure.text: Tasks that are emotionally loaded or difficult

difficult_points: that 从句修饰 tasks，loaded 表示带有情绪负担。
such as 后接动名词短语举例说明任务。

sentences.sentence: People with low self-esteem are more likely to procrastinate.
sentences.translation: 自尊水平低的人更可能拖延。
difficult_points: with low self-esteem 后置修饰 people。
be more likely to 表示可能性更高。

sentences.sentence: Another group of people who tend to procrastinate are perfectionists, who worry their work will be judged harshly by others.
sentences.translation: 另一类容易拖延的人是完美主义者，他们担心自己的工作会受到他人严厉评判。

structure.text: Another group of people who tend to procrastinate
who worry their work will be judged harshly by others

difficult_points: 第一个 who 修饰 people，第二个 who 修饰 perfectionists。
will be judged 是将来被动语态，说明担心受到他人评价。

sentences.sentence: We know that if we don't finish that report or complete those home repairs, then what we did can't be evaluated.
sentences.translation: 我们知道，如果不完成那份报告或那些家务维修，那么我们做过的事情就无法被评估。

structure.text: that if we don't finish that report or complete those home repairs, then what we did can't be evaluated
if we don't finish that report or complete those home repairs

difficult_points: that 从句内嵌 if 条件句；or 连接 finish 与 complete。
what we did 是主语从句，表示“我们做过的事情”。

sentences.sentence: When we avoid such tasks, we also avoid the negative emotions associated with them.
sentences.translation: 当我们逃避这类任务时，也就逃避了与之相关的负面情绪。

structure.text: the negative emotions associated with them

difficult_points: associated with them 是过去分词短语，修饰 emotions。
them 指 such tasks。

sentences.sentence: This is rewarding, and it conditions us to use procrastination to repair our mood.
sentences.translation: 这会带来奖赏，并使我们形成用拖延来修复情绪的习惯。

structure.text: and it conditions us to use procrastination to repair our mood
to use procrastination to repair our mood

difficult_points: it 指逃避负面情绪这一行为；condition someone to do 表示使某人形成习惯。
to repair our mood 表示使用拖延的目的。

sentences.sentence: If we engage in more enjoyable tasks instead, we get another mood boost.
sentences.translation: 如果我们转而做更有趣的任务，就会再次获得情绪上的提升。

structure.text: If we engage in more enjoyable tasks instead

difficult_points: instead 表示转而做其他任务；engage in 后接名词。
mood boost 是情绪改善的比喻表达。

sentences.sentence: In the long run, however, procrastination isn't an effective way of managing emotions.
sentences.translation: 然而从长远看，拖延并不是管理情绪的有效方式。

structure.text: isn't an effective way of managing emotions

difficult_points: in the long run 表示长期结果，与短期 mood boost 对比。
of managing emotions 说明 way 的用途。

sentences.sentence: The 'mood repair' we experience is temporary.
sentences.translation: 我们体验到的“情绪修复”是暂时的。
difficult_points: we experience 是省略关系代词的定语从句，修饰 mood repair。
temporary 表明拖延带来的情绪改善不会持久。

sentences.sentence: Afterwards, people tend to be left with a sense of guilt that not only increases their negative mood, but also reinforces their tendency to procrastinate.
sentences.translation: 之后，人们往往会留下内疚感；这种感觉不仅加重负面情绪，还强化拖延倾向。

structure.text: tend to be left with a sense of guilt
that not only increases their negative mood, but also reinforces their tendency to procrastinate

difficult_points: be left with 表示最终留下某种感受；that 修饰 sense of guilt。
not only...but also... 连接 increases 和 reinforces。

sentences.sentence: So why is this such a problem?
sentences.translation: 那么，这为什么会成为如此严重的问题呢？
difficult_points: such a problem 表示程度很高的“如此严重的问题”。
this 指前文拖延及其情绪后果。

sentences.sentence: When most people think of the costs of procrastination, they think of the toll on productivity.
sentences.translation: 大多数人想到拖延的代价时，会想到它对生产力造成的损耗。

structure.text: When most people think of the costs of procrastination

difficult_points: 两个 think of 分别表示想到“代价”和具体损耗。
toll on productivity 指对效率造成的损失。

sentences.sentence: For example, studies have shown that procrastination negatively impacts student performance.
sentences.translation: 例如，研究表明拖延会对学生表现产生负面影响。

structure.text: that procrastination negatively impacts student performance

difficult_points: 现在完成时 have shown 表示已有研究证据。
negatively 修饰 impacts，说明影响方向。

sentences.sentence: But putting off reading textbooks and writing essays may affect other areas of students' lives.
sentences.translation: 但推迟阅读教材和写论文，也可能影响学生生活的其他方面。

structure.text: putting off reading textbooks and writing essays

difficult_points: putting off 后并列两个动名词 reading 和 writing。
may affect 表示可能造成的扩散影响。

sentences.sentence: In one study of over 3,000 German students over a six-month period, those who reported procrastinating over their university work were also more likely to engage in study-related misconduct, such as cheating and plagiarism.
sentences.translation: 一项对3000多名德国学生进行、历时六个月的研究发现，报告在大学学习中拖延的学生也更可能从事作弊、抄袭等与学习有关的不当行为。

structure.text: In one study of over 3,000 German students over a six-month period
those who reported procrastinating over their university work
who reported procrastinating over their university work
were also more likely to engage in study-related misconduct

difficult_points: those who... 指报告拖延的学生；over a six-month period 表示研究时长。
engage in 后接 misconduct，such as 列举具体行为。

sentences.sentence: But the behaviour that procrastination was most closely linked with was using fraudulent excuses to get deadline extensions.
sentences.translation: 但与拖延关系最密切的行为，是使用虚假借口来获得截止日期延期。

structure.text: the behaviour that procrastination was most closely linked with
that procrastination was most closely linked with
using fraudulent excuses to get deadline extensions

difficult_points: that 从句末尾介词 with 提前关系，修饰 behaviour。
using 动名词作表语；to get 表示使用借口的目的。

sentences.sentence: Other research shows that employees on average spend almost a quarter of their workday procrastinating, and again this is linked with negative outcomes.
sentences.translation: 其他研究显示，员工平均会把近四分之一的工作日耗在拖延上，而这同样与负面结果有关。

structure.text: that employees on average spend almost a quarter of their workday procrastinating
and again this is linked with negative outcomes

difficult_points: spend time doing 表示把时间花在拖延上。
this 指员工工作日中拖延的现象。

sentences.sentence: In fact, in one US survey of over 22,000 employees, participants who said they regularly procrastinated had less annual income and less employment stability.
sentences.translation: 事实上，在一项针对22000多名员工的美国调查中，称自己经常拖延的参与者年收入更低，就业稳定性也更差。

structure.text: In fact, in one US survey of over 22,000 employees
participants who said they regularly procrastinated
had less annual income and less employment stability

difficult_points: who 从句修饰 participants；said 后接省略 that 的宾语从句。
less...and less... 并列比较收入和稳定性。

sentences.sentence: For every one-point increase on a measure of chronic procrastination, annual income decreased by US$15,000.
sentences.translation: 慢性拖延程度每增加一分，年收入就减少15000美元。

structure.text: For every one-point increase on a measure of chronic procrastination

difficult_points: For every... 表示每增加一个单位所对应的变化。
decreased by 表示减少的幅度。

sentences.sentence: Procrastination also correlates with serious health and well-being problems.
sentences.translation: 拖延还与严重的健康和福祉问题相关。
difficult_points: correlate with 表示相关，不等同于直接因果。
well-being 与 health 并列，指整体福祉。

sentences.sentence: A tendency to procrastinate is linked to poor mental health, including higher levels of depression and anxiety.
sentences.translation: 拖延倾向与较差的心理健康有关，包括更高程度的抑郁和焦虑。

structure.text: including higher levels of depression and anxiety

difficult_points: to procrastinate 修饰 tendency，说明倾向内容。
including 短语列出 poor mental health 的具体表现。

sentences.sentence: Across numerous studies, I've found people who regularly procrastinate report a greater number of health issues, such as headaches, flu and colds, and digestive issues.
sentences.translation: 在大量研究中，我发现经常拖延的人报告的健康问题更多，例如头痛、流感和感冒以及消化问题。

structure.text: people who regularly procrastinate report a greater number of health issues
such as headaches, flu and colds, and digestive issues

difficult_points: found 后是省略 that 的宾语从句，people 后省略了 that，构成感知结构。
such as 列举 health issues 的具体种类。

sentences.sentence: They also experience higher levels of stress and poor sleep quality.
sentences.translation: 他们还会经历更高程度的压力和较差的睡眠质量。

structure.text: higher levels of stress and poor sleep quality

difficult_points: They 指经常拖延的人。
higher levels 与 poor sleep quality 并列作 experience 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: They are less likely to practise healthy behaviours, such as eating a healthy diet and regularly exercising, and use destructive coping strategies to manage their stress.
sentences.translation: 他们不太可能保持健康行为，例如健康饮食和规律锻炼，反而会使用有害的应对策略来管理压力。

structure.text: are less likely to practise healthy behaviours
such as eating a healthy diet and regularly exercising
and use destructive coping strategies to manage their stress

difficult_points: are less likely to practise 与 use 并列，主语共享。
such as 后并列 eating 与 exercising；to manage 表示使用策略的目的。

sentences.sentence: In one study of over 700 people, I found people prone to procrastination had a 63% greater risk of poor heart health after accounting for other personality traits and demographics.
sentences.translation: 在一项超过700人的研究中，我发现容易拖延的人心脏健康不佳的风险高出63%，这一结果已考虑其他人格特征和人口统计因素。

structure.text: In one study of over 700 people
people prone to procrastination had a 63% greater risk of poor heart health
after accounting for other personality traits and demographics

difficult_points: found 后省略 that；prone to procrastination 后置修饰 people。
after accounting for 表示在统计控制其他因素之后。

sentences.sentence: Finding better ways of managing our emotions is one route out of the vicious cycle of procrastination.
sentences.translation: 寻找更好的情绪管理方式，是摆脱拖延恶性循环的一条途径。

structure.text: Finding better ways of managing our emotions
one route out of the vicious cycle of procrastination

difficult_points: Finding... 是动名词短语作主语，of managing 修饰 ways。
route out of 表示摆脱某种困境的途径。

sentences.sentence: An important first step is to manage our environment and how we view the task.
sentences.translation: 重要的第一步是管理我们的环境以及我们看待任务的方式。

structure.text: to manage our environment and how we view the task

difficult_points: to manage 是表语不定式，说明 first step 的内容。
how 从句与 our environment 并列作 manage 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: There are a number of evidence-based strategies that can help us fend off distractions that can occupy our minds when we should be focusing on the thing we should be getting on with.
sentences.translation: 有许多基于证据的策略，可以帮助我们抵御那些在我们本应专注于手头任务时占据心思的干扰。

structure.text: that can help us fend off distractions
that can occupy our minds when we should be focusing on the thing we should be getting on with
when we should be focusing on the thing we should be getting on with
we should be getting on with

difficult_points: 连续两个 that 从句分别修饰 strategies 和 distractions。
when 从句中 the thing 后又省略关系代词，get on with 表示处理手头任务。

sentences.sentence: For example, reminding ourselves about why the task is important and valuable can increase positive feelings towards it.
sentences.translation: 例如，提醒自己任务为何重要且有价值，可以增强我们对它的积极感受。

structure.text: reminding ourselves about why the task is important and valuable
why the task is important and valuable

difficult_points: reminding ourselves 是动名词主语；why 从句作 about 的宾语。
towards it 中 it 指 task。

sentences.sentence: Forgiving ourselves and feeling compassion when we procrastinate can help break the procrastination cycle.
sentences.translation: 拖延时原谅自己并对自己怀有同情，有助于打破拖延循环。

structure.text: Forgiving ourselves and feeling compassion when we procrastinate

difficult_points: 两个动名词 forgiving 和 feeling 并列作主语。
help 后可接不带 to 的 break；when 从句修饰 feeling compassion。

sentences.sentence: We should admit that we feel bad, but not be overly critical of ourselves.
sentences.translation: 我们应该承认自己感觉不好，但不要过度苛责自己。

structure.text: but not be overly critical of ourselves

difficult_points: that 从句说明 admit 的内容；but 后省略主语和 should。
be critical of 表示批评、苛责，overly 表示过度。

sentences.sentence: We should remind ourselves that we're not the first person to procrastinate, nor the last.
sentences.translation: 我们应该提醒自己，我们不是第一个拖延的人，也不会是最后一个。

structure.text: that we're not the first person to procrastinate, nor the last

difficult_points: that 从句中 the last 后省略了 person to procrastinate。
nor 连接否定并列，表示“也不是”。

sentences.sentence: Doing this can take the edge off the negative feelings we have about ourselves when we procrastinate.
sentences.translation: 这样做可以缓解我们拖延时对自己的负面感受。

structure.text: the negative feelings we have about ourselves when we procrastinate

difficult_points: Doing this 指前面自我提醒和宽容的做法。
take the edge off 是固定表达，we have 修饰 feelings。

sentences.sentence: This can all make it easier to get back on track.
sentences.translation: 这些做法都能让我们更容易回到正轨。

structure.text: it easier to get back on track

difficult_points: make it easier to do 是形式宾语结构，it 指 to get back on track。
This 指前文所有情绪管理策略，all 表示总的效果。
